title: Climate-Resilient Seed Access And Adoption Gaps In Smallholder Agriculture
summary: Build a verified workflow for mapping climate-resilient seed availability and estimating adoption gaps in smallholder agriculture, with explicit seed-system barrier classification, on-farm-yield-gap documentation, and farmer-preference context.
why_it_matters: Fewer than 10 percent of smallholder farmers in Sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia use improved climate-resilient seed varieties despite decades of breeding-program investment. The bottleneck is not genetic — climate-resilient varieties exist in breeder collections — but delivery: seed multiplication, distribution, last-mile retail, farmer awareness, affordability, and trust. Without verified adoption-gap analysis, seed-system investments cannot be targeted where they would most increase climate resilience for the farmers who need it most.

success_metrics.target: Every adoption-gap claim links to seed-availability data, farmer-survey data, or seed-system indicator with source, date, and geographic grain.
success_metrics.validation_method: Schema validation and reviewer spot-checks against TASAI, CGIAR, and national agricultural survey documentation.

success_metrics.target: No climate-resilient seed effectiveness claim is merged without documenting the difference between on-station trial yields and on-farm yields under actual farmer conditions.
success_metrics.validation_method: Reviewer confirms yield-gap documentation for every crop-country combination where effectiveness is claimed.

success_metrics.target: Every adoption-gap analysis documents which seed-system barriers (multiplication, distribution, awareness, affordability, trust) are most tractable for the analyzed context.
success_metrics.validation_method: Red-team reviewer confirms barrier classification methodology and tractability assessment.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, yield-gap safety review, and replication for quantitative outputs.
